codeReview.summary: Feature orch-novelty-check is properly implemented and fully wired. NoveltyChecker is integrated into Orchestrator::create_task and create_task_with_autonomy. The module uses Levenshtein distance for description similarity (>85%) and set intersection for file overlap (>80%). Error types DuplicateTask and DuplicateTaskByFileOverlap are properly defined in swell-core. 24 tests pass. Clippy passes. No hardcoded test doubles in production code paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff commitId (fb45cad) references a different feature (value_scorer complexity) than the novelty-check-wiring feature being reviewed. The actual novelty-check implementation was committed earlier in 5585794. This is a minor handoff metadata issue - the code itself is correct.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file says commitId='fb45cad' but git show fb45cad shows value_scorer changes. The novelty_check.rs was added in commit 5585794.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker session feature ID ('novelty-check-wiring') differs from canonical feature ID in features.json ('orch-novelty-check'). This appears to be a naming convention difference rather than an error.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff and transcript skeleton use featureId='novelty-check-wiring' while features.json uses id='orch-novelty-check'

summary: PASS - Feature orch-novelty-check is properly implemented. NoveltyChecker module (novelty_check.rs) provides duplicate task detection via Levenshtein similarity (>85%) and file overlap (>80%). It is fully wired into Orchestrator::create_task and create_task_with_autonomy with proper error types (DuplicateTask, DuplicateTaskByFileOverlap). 24 unit tests pass. Clippy passes with no warnings. No hardcoded MockLlm or test doubles in production paths.
